sanskrit: यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च । न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पार्थ  दुष्ट बुद्धिवाला मनुष्य जिस धृतिके द्वारा निद्रा, भय, चिन्ता, दुःख और घमण्डको भी नहीं छोड़ता, वह धृति तामसी है ॥
englishTranslation: The dull-minded one does not let go of sleep, fear, grief, sorrow, and pride. That steadfastness is tamasic, Arjuna.
essence: Clinging to fear and sorrow is not strength; it is tamasic inertia.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Some people hold on to sleepiness, fear, grief, despair, and pride instead of releasing them. That kind of stubbornness is dull and tamasic.
simpleInsight: Not every kind of firmness is strength; some of it is just clinging to what keeps you heavy.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield at Kurukshetra, Krishna is classifying the kinds of resolve that drive action. After describing sattvic and rajasic steadfastness, he names the lowest form: the mind that cannot release fear, grief, sorrow, sleep, or pride.
modernRelevance: You keep replaying the same failure in bed at 2 a.m., feeding the fear instead of setting it down. Krishna calls that not strength, but heaviness.
translationLiteral: यया (by which) / स्वप्नम् (sleep) / भयम् (fear) / शोकम् (grief) / विषादम् (sorrow) / मदम् (pride) / एव (even) / च (and) / न विमुञ्चति (does not let go) / दुर्मेधाः (the dull-minded one) / धृतिः (steadfastness) / सा (that) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / तामसी (tamasic)
